domain.name: field_service
domain.description: Field service & ops dispatch (use case #10). A DispatchAgent grounds on an inbound ServiceRequest, the reported Symptoms, the Asset's telemetry/service history, and the Part catalog, then PROPOSES a diagnosis + a parts list + a work order. The bounded action space the agent proposes is: diagnose | parts-lookup | create-work-order. Diagnosis and parts lookup are auto (read/propose only); the two write steps that touch the physical/financial world — dispatching a technician and committing parts inventory — require a dispatcher to approve in an OutSystems approval workflow (BPT) before they land. The agent never dispatches a technician or commits parts directly — it proposes a DispatchProposal that a human signs off. Every action is audited. The bounded action space + the human-at-the-write-step is what makes it ship, not just reason.

capabilities.name: LogServiceRequest
capabilities.note: Public intake — logs the request + reported symptoms against an asset and kicks off diagnosis + dispatch.

capabilities.name: DiagnoseAndPropose
capabilities.note: THE AGENT. Grounds on the ServiceRequest + reported Symptoms + Asset service history (telemetry tool) + the Part catalog (parts-lookup tool). Bounded action space it PROPOSES: diagnose | parts-lookup | create-work-order — it writes a Diagnosis, drafts a WorkOrder, and PROPOSES a DispatchProposal (proposed technician + parts + RiskTier). It NEVER dispatches a technician or commits parts inventory directly — those are held for a dispatcher's approval.

capabilities.name: DispatchTechnician
capabilities.note: The bounded WRITE to the system of record: assign the proposed technician, mark the DispatchProposal executed + the WorkOrder dispatched, flip the tech unavailable, audited. Called by the workflow only AFTER a dispatcher approves.

capabilities.name: CommitParts
capabilities.note: The other bounded write: decrement Part QtyOnHand for the approved parts list (commit inventory), audited. Also gated behind dispatcher approval — the agent may only PROPOSE the parts, never commit them.

capabilities.name: DispatchWorkflow
capabilities.note: HITL via OutSystems BPT: on ServiceRequestLogged the workflow runs the DispatchAgent to produce a Diagnosis (diagnose step) and then a proposed WorkOrder + DispatchProposal (parts-lookup step). It then HOLDS at a Human Approval activity — a dispatcher approves the proposal — and only THEN calls DispatchTechnician (assign + dispatch) and CommitParts (commit inventory). Diagnosis + parts lookup are auto; the two writes are dispatcher-approved. The approval + audit is native workflow, not custom code.

capabilities.name: DispatcherInbox
capabilities.note: Where a dispatcher approves/rejects a held DispatchProposal — the write-step sign-off before DispatchTechnician + CommitParts run.

capabilities.name: ManageCatalog
capabilities.note: Admin maintains the Asset registry + Part catalog the agent grounds on.

integrations.name: Telemetry
integrations.purpose: IoT / device telemetry source (REST) the DispatchAgent can retrieve asset health + fault codes from as a grounding tool

integrations.name: InventorySystem
integrations.purpose: external inventory / warehouse system reconciled on CommitParts to reserve and draw down stock

integrations.name: Notify
integrations.purpose: customer / technician notifications on dispatch or completion
